meaning: n.(尤指介于一层与二层之间的)中层楼,(戏院中的)包厢
examples: Each has a snug mezzanine area and an antler-fenced balcony about eight metres above ground.
He came from the twenty-third floor down to the lobby on the mezzanine to collect his mail before breakfast, and he believed—he hoped—that he looked passably well: doing all right.
Or you have to attend the fashion show at the shopping center atrium and throw strawberry gelatin from the mezzanine.
One entrepreneur told Baier that none of the investment banks or mezzanine investors would even return his calls.
Some provide early stage (seed) capital, while others come in with mezzanine or growth capital.
